faculty.summary: Works on machine learning for healthcare, including predictive models, causal inference, and safe and reliable AI in medicine.

faculty.name: Tianmin Shu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on AI and machine social intelligence, including theory of mind, multi-agent systems, and human-AI collaboration.

faculty.name: Jessica Sorrell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the theoretical foundations of machine learning, including reproducibility, stability, and privacy.

faculty.name: Mathias Unberath
faculty.title: John C. Malone Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computer vision and machine learning for medical imaging, including image-guided procedures and augmented reality.

faculty.name: Ziang Xiao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works at the intersection of human-computer interaction and NLP, including conversational AI and evaluation of language technologies.

faculty.name: Yaxing Yao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Researches usable privacy and human-computer interaction, including privacy in emerging technologies such as AR/VR.

faculty.name: Lydia Zakynthinou
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Works on the theoretical foundations of machine learning, including differential privacy and statistical learning.

faculty.name: Renjie Zhao
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor working on wireless and mobile systems, including sensing, networking, and next-generation wireless.

funding.note: Fully funded (all Rossin College PhDs 100% funded); P3 Fellowship available

faculty.name: Brian D. Davison
faculty.title: Professor and Chair; Daniel E. '39 and Patricia M. Smith Endowed Chair, Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Department Chair and leading researcher in data science, web search, and information retrieval. Work on machine learning and data mining with applications in text, the Web, and social networks. Co-founder of the MS in Data Science program. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Daniel P. Lopresti
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Interim Dean, Rossin College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Distinguished researcher in pattern recognition and document analysis systems. Work on electronic voting security, cybersecurity, and bioinformatics. Interim Dean of the Rossin College of Engineering and Applied Science. ACM Distinguished Scientist.

faculty.name: Mooi Choo Chuah
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: IEEE Fellow and NAI Fellow. Leading researcher in network and cyber-physical system security, mobile healthcare, and assistive technologies. Holds 62+ US patents. Previously Distinguished Member of Technical Staff at Bell Labs. Associate Director of I-DISC.

faculty.name: Jeff Heflin
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director, Cognitive Science Program
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in artificial intelligence, the Semantic Web, and knowledge representation. Work on ontologies, intelligent agents, and web-based knowledge systems. Director of the Cognitive Science Program.

faculty.name: Henry Korth
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in database systems, blockchain technology, and distributed systems. Work on zero-knowledge proofs, consensus algorithms, and blockchain benchmarking. Co-director of the Computer Science and Business (CSB) program. Co-author of the widely-used 'Database System Concepts' textbook.

faculty.name: Michael Spear
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Director of Master's Program
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in concurrency, parallel computing, and programming language design. Work on transactional memory, multicore synchronization, and cloud computing infrastructure. Director of the MS program in CSE.

faculty.name: Parv Venkitasubramaniam
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in privacy and security, with focus on the fundamental tradeoffs between privacy and performance in networked systems. Work on information-theoretic privacy, cyber-physical security, and statistical data privacy.

faculty.name: Roberto Palmieri
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in distributed computing and systems. Work on concurrent data structures, transactional memory, and fault-tolerant distributed protocols. Focus on cluster and geographic replication for high-performance and reliable systems.

faculty.name: Eric P. S. Baumer
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in human-computer interaction and social informatics. NSF CAREER awardee. Work on participatory methods for human-centered design of algorithmic systems, natural language processing, and critical studies of algorithms in society.

faculty.name: Hector Munoz-Avila
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: NSF CAREER awardee. Researcher in agentic AI, hierarchical task network (HTN) planning, and case-based reasoning. Current work on AI grounding, AI alignment, and combining hierarchical representations with large language models. Lehigh Class of 1961 Professor.

faculty.name: Lifang He
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in machine learning and data science. Work on tensor analysis methods and their applications to biomedical informatics and neuroimaging. IEEE Senior Member. Tenured in 2025. Multiple NSF, NIH, and DOE grants.

faculty.name: Brian Y. Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; PhD Program Director
faculty.summary: Researcher in structural bioinformatics and computational biology. Work on algorithms for protein structure analysis and biomolecular recognition. Director of the PhD program in CSE. Bridges computer science, biology, and chemistry.

faculty.name: Aparna Bharati
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in computer vision and multimedia forensics. Work on deep learning methods for image manipulation detection, biometrics, and media integrity verification. Leads the Lehigh Computer Vision Group (LCVG). PhD from Notre Dame (2020).

faculty.name: David Saldaña
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Rising researcher in AI and robotics. Work on autonomous aerial robots, resilient robot swarms, and environmental monitoring applications. Focus on making robot teams robust to failures in challenging environments.

faculty.name: Sihong Xie
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Researcher in data mining and machine learning. Work on crowdsourcing, text and opinion mining, and mining noisy and biased data. Focus on making data-driven systems more reliable and trustworthy.

funding.note: Fully funded (full tuition, stipend, medical insurance)

faculty.name: Leslie Kaelbling
faculty.title: Panasonic Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Leader in AI, reinforcement learning, and robot learning. Panasonic Professor and MIT AI+D faculty member. Research spans planning under uncertainty, sensorimotor learning, and foundational AI.

faculty.name: Daniela Rus
faculty.title: Director of CSAIL; Andrew (1956) and Erna Viterbi Professor
faculty.summary: Director of MIT CSAIL. Research in robotics, AI, and autonomous systems. Leads work on soft robotics, self-driving vehicles, and distributed robotic networks.

faculty.name: Antonio Torralba
faculty.title: Delta Electronics Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Torralba, faculty head of AI and Decision-making, works on computer vision and machine learning, including scene understanding and multimodal perception.

faculty.name: Regina Barzilay
faculty.title: School of Engineering Distinguished Professor for AI and Health
faculty.summary: Barzilay works on natural language processing and machine learning, with major applications to clinical AI, diagnosis, and molecular and drug discovery.

faculty.name: Tommi Jaakkola
faculty.title: Thomas M. Siebel Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor in statistical machine learning, inference, and ML for molecular design. Work spans generative models, adversarial robustness, and computational biology.

faculty.name: Aleksander Mądry
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in trustworthy AI and ML theory. Faculty lead of the Center for Deployable Machine Learning. Research in robustness, fairness, and foundations of modern ML paradigms.

faculty.name: Kaiming He
faculty.title: Douglas Ross Career Development Professor, Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: He works on computer vision and deep learning, known for foundational neural-network architectures and self-supervised representation learning.

faculty.name: Constantinos Daskalakis
faculty.title: Armen Avanessians Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Daskalakis works on theoretical computer science at the interface of game theory, economics, probability, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Erik Demaine
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Renowned algorithms researcher, MacArthur Fellow. Research in computational origami, data structures, graph algorithms, and combinatorial geometry. Widely published across theory and recreational mathematics.

faculty.name: Piotr Indyk
faculty.title: Thomas D. and Virginia W. Cabot Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Indyk works on algorithms for high-dimensional and massive data, including nearest-neighbor search, sketching, and sparse recovery.

faculty.name: Ankur Moitra
faculty.title: Norbert Wiener Professor of Mathematics and Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Moitra works on algorithms and the theoretical foundations of machine learning, including provable methods for high-dimensional inference.

faculty.name: Samuel Madden
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor and leader in database systems, big data infrastructure, and ML systems. Research in data management for analytics, cloud computing, and IoT data platforms.

faculty.name: Hari Balakrishnan
faculty.title: Fujitsu Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Balakrishnan works on networked and mobile computer systems, including wireless networks, sensing, and data systems.

faculty.name: Dina Katabi
faculty.title: Thuan and Nicole Pham Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Katabi works on wireless networks and sensing, including using radio signals and machine learning to monitor health and human activity.

faculty.name: M. Frans Kaashoek
faculty.title: Charles A. Piper Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kaashoek works on operating systems and distributed systems, including systems design and the verification of systems software.

faculty.name: Barbara Liskov
faculty.title: Institute Professor
faculty.summary: Institute Professor and Turing Award winner (2008). Inventor of the Liskov substitution principle, a foundational concept in object-oriented programming. Pioneered abstract data types and distributed computing.

faculty.name: Tim Kraska
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kraska works on data systems, including learned database components and the integration of machine learning with data management.

faculty.name: Srini Devadas
faculty.title: Edwin Sibley Webster Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in computer security, hardware security, and cryptography. Research in secure processors, trusted computing, and cryptographic protocols. ACM Fellow.

faculty.name: Vinod Vaikuntanathan
faculty.title: Ford Foundation Professor of Engineering, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Vaikuntanathan works on cryptography, including lattice-based and fully homomorphic encryption and the theoretical foundations of secure computation.

faculty.name: Yael Kalai
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leading researcher in cryptography and computational complexity. Research focuses on succinct proofs, delegation of computation, and foundational cryptographic constructions. Invited speaker at ICM.

faculty.name: Pulkit Agrawal
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Agrawal works on machine learning for robotics and embodied intelligence, including reinforcement learning and perception for robots.

faculty.name: Jacob Andreas
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Andreas works on natural language processing and machine learning, including grounded language understanding and using language to guide learning.

faculty.name: Stefanie Jegelka
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Jegelka works on machine learning, including optimization, learning on graphs and structured data, and the theory of neural representations.

faculty.name: David Sontag
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leader in probabilistic graphical models, causal inference, and AI for healthcare. Research applies ML to clinical decision support, disease progression modeling, and health policy.

faculty.name: Mohammad Alizadeh
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Alizadeh works on computer networks and systems, including datacenter networking, congestion control, and applying machine learning to systems problems.

faculty.name: Harold Abelson
faculty.title: Class of 1922 Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Abelson works on computing and education, co-authoring foundational programming texts and creating platforms such as MIT App Inventor for accessible software creation.

faculty.name: Elfar Adalsteinsson
faculty.title: Eaton-Peabody Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Adalsteinsson develops magnetic resonance imaging methods and hardware, including techniques for high-field and fetal MRI.

faculty.name: Akintunde I. Akinwande
faculty.title: Thomas and Gerd Perkins Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Akinwande researches micro- and nano-fabricated electronic and field-emission devices, including displays and vacuum microelectronics.

faculty.name: Saman P. Amarasinghe
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Amarasinghe works on compilers and programming systems, including domain-specific languages and automatic optimization for high-performance computing.

faculty.name: Marc A. Baldo
faculty.title: Dugald C. Jackson Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Baldo researches organic and excitonic optoelectronic devices, including approaches to solar energy conversion and light emission.

faculty.name: Karl K. Berggren
faculty.title: Julius A. Stratton Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Berggren works on quantum nanostructures and superconducting electronics, including nanofabrication and single-photon detectors.

faculty.name: Sangeeta N. Bhatia
faculty.title: John J. and Dorothy Wilson Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Bhatia works at the interface of engineering and medicine, developing micro- and nanotechnologies for tissue engineering, diagnostics, and cancer therapy.

faculty.name: Duane S. Boning
faculty.title: Clarence J. LeBel Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Boning researches manufacturing and process control for semiconductors and nanomanufacturing, including modeling and control of process variation.

faculty.name: Vladimir Bulović
faculty.title: Fariborz Maseeh Professor of Emerging Technology
faculty.summary: Bulović, who directs MIT.nano, researches organic and nanostructured thin-film electronic and optoelectronic devices, including flexible electronics and solar cells.

faculty.name: Anantha P. Chandrakasan
faculty.title: Vannevar Bush Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Chandrakasan works on energy-efficient integrated circuits and systems, including ultra-low-power design for wireless sensing and IoT devices.

faculty.name: Adam Chlipala
faculty.title: Arthur J. Conner Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Chlipala works on programming languages and formal verification, using proof assistants to build verified software and hardware systems.

faculty.name: Isaac Chuang
faculty.title: Julius A. Stratton Professor of Electrical Engineering and Physics
faculty.summary: Chuang is a pioneer of quantum information science, working on quantum computing architectures, algorithms, and experimental quantum systems.

faculty.name: Munther A. Dahleh
faculty.title: William A. Coolidge Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Dahleh, director of the Institute for Data, Systems, and Society, works on control theory, networked systems, and the science of large-scale sociotechnical systems.

faculty.name: Luca Daniel
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Daniel works on numerical simulation and design automation for circuits and systems, including uncertainty quantification and robustness of machine-learning models.

faculty.name: Randall Davis
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Davis works on artificial intelligence and knowledge-based systems, including reasoning and multimodal human-computer interaction.

faculty.name: Jesús A. del Alamo
faculty.title: Donner Professor of Science, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Del Alamo researches semiconductor electronic devices, including compound-semiconductor and nanoscale transistors for future electronics.

faculty.name: Erik D. Demaine
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Demaine works on algorithms and computational geometry, including data structures and the mathematics of folding and reconfiguration.

faculty.name: Srinivas Devadas
faculty.title: Edwin Sibley Webster Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Devadas works on computer architecture and security, including secure processors, hardware security primitives, and computer-aided design.

faculty.name: Frederic Durand
faculty.title: Amar Bose Professor of Computing
faculty.summary: Durand works on computer graphics and computational photography, including rendering, image processing, and computational imaging.

faculty.name: Dirk R. Englund
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Englund researches quantum photonic devices and systems for quantum computing, communication, and sensing.

faculty.name: Dennis M. Freeman
faculty.title: Henry Ellis Warren Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Freeman studies the micromechanics of hearing and biological signal processing, using microscopy to understand the mechanics of the inner ear.

faculty.name: James G. Fujimoto
faculty.title: Elihu Thomson Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Fujimoto is a co-inventor of optical coherence tomography and works on biomedical optics and imaging systems for medical diagnostics.

faculty.name: Polina Golland
faculty.title: Sunlin and Priscilla Chou Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Golland develops machine-learning and computer-vision methods for medical image analysis and biological data.

faculty.name: Martha L. Gray
faculty.title: J. W. Kieckhefer Professor / Whitaker Professor in Biomedical Engineering
faculty.summary: Gray works on biomedical engineering and imaging, including diagnostic imaging of tissue and translational health technology.

faculty.name: W. Eric L. Grimson
faculty.title: Bernard M. Gordon Professor of Medical Engineering, Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Grimson works on computer vision and its application to medical image analysis and activity recognition.

faculty.name: John V. Guttag
faculty.title: Dugald C. Jackson Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Guttag works on machine learning and data-driven methods applied to medicine, health, and other domains.

faculty.name: Jongyoon Han
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and Biological Engineering
faculty.summary: Han works on micro- and nanofluidic systems for biological and biomedical applications, including molecular separation and sample preparation.

faculty.name: Ruonan Han
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Han researches terahertz and millimeter-wave integrated circuits for sensing, communication, and security applications.

faculty.name: Thomas Heldt
faculty.title: Richard J. Cohen Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Heldt works on computational physiology and medical devices, developing model-based estimation from physiological signals for clinical monitoring.

faculty.name: Qing Hu
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Hu researches terahertz photonics and quantum electronic devices, including quantum cascade lasers and terahertz imaging.

faculty.name: Daniel Huttenlocher
faculty.title: Henry Ellis Warren Professor; Dean, MIT Schwarzman College of Computing
faculty.sub_field: Computer vision and computing and society
faculty.summary: Huttenlocher, dean of the Schwarzman College of Computing, works on computer vision and the interplay of computing, AI, and society.

faculty.name: Tommi S. Jaakkola
faculty.title: Thomas M. Siebel Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Jaakkola works on machine learning theory and methods, including generative models and applications to molecular and scientific problems.

faculty.name: Patrick Jaillet
faculty.title: Dugald C. Jackson Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Jaillet works on optimization and operations research, including online and stochastic optimization and decision-making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Leslie P. Kaelbling
faculty.title: Panasonic Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Kaelbling works on artificial intelligence and robotics, including learning, planning, and decision-making for robots acting in the physical world.

faculty.name: Yael Tauman Kalai
faculty.title: Ellen Swallow Richards Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kalai works on cryptography and theoretical computer science, including verifiable delegation of computation and the foundations of proofs.

faculty.name: David R. Karger
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Karger works on algorithms and information management, including randomized algorithms and systems and interfaces for organizing personal information.

faculty.name: Manolis Kellis
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kellis works on computational biology and genomics, including comparative and regulatory genomics and the genetics of disease.

faculty.name: Leslie A. Kolodziejski
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Kolodziejski researches photonic and optoelectronic materials and devices, including epitaxial growth and fabrication of semiconductor structures.

faculty.name: Jing Kong
faculty.title: Jerry McAfee (1940) Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kong researches the synthesis and applications of low-dimensional nanomaterials, including graphene and other two-dimensional materials.

faculty.name: Jeffrey H. Lang
faculty.title: Vitesse Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Lang works on the analysis and design of electromechanical and power-electronic systems, including micro-scale actuators and energy conversion.

faculty.name: Hae-Seung Lee
faculty.title: Advanced Television and Signal Processing Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Lee researches analog and mixed-signal integrated circuits, including high-performance data converters and sensor interface electronics.

faculty.name: Steven B. Leeb
faculty.title: Emanuel E. Landsman Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leeb works on power electronics and nonintrusive load monitoring, developing sensing and control for electromechanical and energy systems.

faculty.name: Charles E. Leiserson
faculty.title: Edwin Sibley Webster Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Leiserson works on parallel computing and algorithms, including cache-efficient algorithms, parallel programming, and software performance engineering.

faculty.name: Tomás Lozano-Pérez
faculty.title: School of Engineering Professor of Teaching Excellence, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Lozano-Pérez works on robotics and artificial intelligence, including motion and task planning and integrated robot decision-making.

faculty.name: Aleksander Madry
faculty.title: Cadence Design Systems Professor of Computing
faculty.summary: Madry works on machine learning and algorithmic optimization, with an emphasis on the reliability, robustness, and understanding of ML systems.

faculty.name: Thomas L. Magnanti
faculty.title: Institute Professor, Professor of Operations Research and Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Magnanti works on large-scale optimization and operations research, including network design and integer and combinatorial optimization.

faculty.name: Wojciech Matusik
faculty.title: Joan and Irwin M. Jacobs Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Matusik works on computer graphics and computational fabrication, developing methods for computational design and manufacturing of physical objects.

faculty.name: Muriel Médard
faculty.title: NEC Professor of Software Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Médard works on information theory and coding, including network coding and algebraic coding methods for communication and storage.

faculty.name: Alexandre Megretski
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Megretski works on control and systems theory, including robust and nonlinear control and computational methods for system analysis.

faculty.name: Robert C. Miller
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Miller works on human-computer interaction and software engineering, including user interfaces, crowd computing, and programming tools.

faculty.name: Robert T. Morris
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Morris works on operating systems, distributed systems, and networking, building experimental systems for scalable and robust computing.

faculty.name: Sendhil Mullainathan
faculty.title: Peter de Florez Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and Economics
faculty.summary: Mullainathan works at the intersection of machine learning and economics, studying algorithmic decision-making, bias, and behavioral phenomena.

faculty.name: William D. Oliver
faculty.title: Henry Ellis Warren Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Oliver leads work on superconducting quantum computing, developing qubits, control, and architectures for scalable quantum processors.

faculty.name: Asuman Ozdaglar
faculty.title: MathWorks Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science; Department Head
faculty.summary: Ozdaglar, head of EECS, works on optimization, game theory, and networked systems, including distributed optimization and the economics of networks.

faculty.name: Tomás Palacios
faculty.title: Clarence J. LeBel Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Palacios, who directs the Microsystems Technology Laboratories, researches electronic devices based on wide-bandgap and two-dimensional materials for power and high-frequency electronics.

faculty.name: Pablo A. Parrilo
faculty.title: Joseph F. and Nancy P. Keithley Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Parrilo works on optimization and control, including semidefinite and polynomial optimization and algebraic methods for engineering systems.

faculty.name: David J. Perreault
faculty.title: Ford Foundation Professor of Engineering, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Perreault researches power electronics and energy conversion, including high-frequency power circuits and architectures.

faculty.name: Yury Polyanskiy
faculty.title: Leverett Howell and William King Cutten Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Polyanskiy works on information theory, including finite-blocklength coding, and connections between information theory and statistics.

faculty.name: Rajeev J. Ram
faculty.title: Clarence J. LeBel Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Ram works on photonics, electronics, and their application to energy, sensing, and biological and chemical microsystems.

faculty.name: L. Rafael Reif
faculty.title: Ray and Maria Stata Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Reif, MIT President Emeritus, works on microelectronics and semiconductor fabrication technologies, including three-dimensional integrated circuits.

faculty.name: Martin C. Rinard
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rinard works on programming systems and software reliability, including program analysis, optimization, and techniques for robust software.

faculty.name: Ronitt Rubinfeld
faculty.title: Edwin Sibley Webster Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rubinfeld works on sublinear-time algorithms and property testing, studying what can be learned from very small samples of large datasets.

faculty.name: Daniela L. Rus
faculty.title: Andrew and Erna Viterbi Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science; Director, CSAIL
faculty.summary: Rus, director of CSAIL, works on robotics, including distributed and soft robotics and machine learning for autonomous systems.

faculty.name: Daniel Sánchez
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Sánchez works on computer architecture, including scalable memory systems, parallel architectures, and hardware for emerging workloads.

faculty.name: Devavrat Shah
faculty.title: Andrew and Erna Viterbi Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Shah works on statistical inference, machine learning, and networks, including algorithms for large-scale data and decision systems.

faculty.name: Nir N. Shavit
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Shavit works on multiprocessor and concurrent computing and, more recently, on connectomics and neural-network computation.

faculty.name: Armando Solar-Lezama
faculty.title: Distinguished Professor of Computing
faculty.summary: Solar-Lezama works on program synthesis and programming languages, developing methods that automatically generate and reason about programs.

faculty.name: David A. Sontag
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Sontag works on machine learning for healthcare, developing methods for clinical prediction, diagnosis, and causal inference from health data.

faculty.name: Collin M. Stultz
faculty.title: Nina T. and Robert H. Rubin Professor in Medical Engineering and Science, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Stultz works on computational biophysics and machine learning for medicine, including protein modeling and clinical prediction for cardiovascular disease.

faculty.name: Gerald Jay Sussman
faculty.title: Panasonic Professor of Electrical Engineering
faculty.summary: Sussman works on artificial intelligence, programming, and symbolic computation, with longstanding contributions to computing education and languages.

faculty.name: Vivienne Sze
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Sze works on energy-efficient computing and hardware for machine learning and computer vision, co-designing algorithms and hardware for efficient inference.

faculty.name: Russell L. Tedrake
faculty.title: Toyota Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Aeronautics and Astronautics, and Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Tedrake works on robotics, control, and optimization, including motion planning, manipulation, and learning for dynamic and dexterous robots.

faculty.name: Bruce Tidor
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and Biological Engineering
faculty.summary: Tidor works on computational biology and molecular design, using modeling and optimization to engineer molecules and understand biological systems.

faculty.name: Caroline Uhler
faculty.title: Andrew and Erna Viterbi Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Uhler works on statistics, machine learning, and their application to genomics, including causal inference and graphical models for biological data.

faculty.name: Joel Voldman
faculty.title: William R. Brody Professor, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Voldman works on bioMEMS and microfluidic systems for manipulating and analyzing cells, including point-of-care and diagnostic devices.

faculty.name: Martin J. Wainwright
faculty.title: Cecil H. Green Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Wainwright works on high-dimensional statistics, machine learning, and optimization, including the statistical and computational foundations of learning.

faculty.name: Jacob K. White
faculty.title: Cecil H. Green Professor in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: White works on numerical simulation and design tools for electronic, biological, and physical systems, including fast solvers for large-scale models.

faculty.name: Ryan Williams
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Williams works on computational complexity and algorithms, including connections between algorithm design and circuit lower bounds.

faculty.name: Virginia Vassilevska Williams
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Vassilevska Williams works on algorithms and complexity, including fast matrix multiplication, graph algorithms, and fine-grained complexity.

faculty.name: Gregory W. Wornell
faculty.title: Sumitomo Electric Industries Professor in Engineering, Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Wornell works on signal processing, information theory, and their applications to communication, sensing, and machine learning systems.

faculty.name: Nickolai Zeldovich
