faculty.summary: Sivaraman works on networked systems, including programmable network hardware and languages and tools for high-performance networking.

faculty.name: Julia Stoyanovich
faculty.title: Institute Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering, Associate Professor of Data Science
faculty.summary: Stoyanovich works on responsible data management and data science, including fairness, transparency, and the ethical use of data and automated decision systems.

faculty.name: Lakshminarayanan Subramanian
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.sub_field: Networked systems and AI for development
faculty.summary: Subramanian works on networked systems and computing for global development, applying networking and machine learning to problems in health, finance, and connectivity.

faculty.name: Torsten Suel
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Suel works on information retrieval and web search, including indexing, query processing, compression, and algorithms for large web-scale data.

faculty.name: Qi Sun
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Sun works on computer graphics and perception for virtual and augmented reality, developing perceptually informed rendering and display techniques.

faculty.name: Joseph Tassarotti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Tassarotti works on programming languages and formal verification, including machine-checked proofs for concurrent and probabilistic programs.

faculty.name: Julian Togelius
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Togelius works on artificial intelligence and games, including procedural content generation, game-playing agents, and AI-assisted game design.

faculty.name: Paul Torrens
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and Engineering; Vice Dean for Faculty Affairs, Tandon School of Engineering
faculty.summary: Torrens works on geographic information science and geosimulation, using agent-based modeling and spatial computing to study human movement and behavior in cities.

faculty.name: Erdem Varol
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Varol develops machine-learning and statistical methods for neuroscience and biological imaging, analyzing high-dimensional neural and biomedical data.

faculty.name: Michael Walfish
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Walfish works on computer systems, including verifiable computation and building systems whose correctness and security can be checked.

faculty.name: Sam Westrick
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Westrick works on programming languages and systems for parallel computing, including functional parallel programming and memory management.

faculty.name: Ed K Wong
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering
faculty.summary: Wong works on image processing, multimedia, and pattern recognition.

faculty.name: Sherry Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Yang works on machine learning for sequential decision making, including generative and foundation models, world models, and reinforcement learning.

faculty.name: Chee Yap
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Yap works on computational geometry and exact geometric and algebraic computation, with applications to robotics and reliable geometric algorithms.

faculty.name: Sai Qian Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Zhang works on efficient machine learning and hardware-software co-design, developing systems and accelerators for machine learning and immersive computing.

faculty.name: Denis Zorin
faculty.title: Silver Professor of Computer Science and Mathematics
faculty.summary: Zorin works on geometric modeling, computer graphics, and scientific computing, including subdivision surfaces, geometry processing, and physical simulation for design and fabrication.

faculty.name: Amal Ahmed
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Amal Ahmed is an associate dean of graduate programs and a professor at Khoury College. Her programming languages research has touched on semantics, type systems, secure compilation, gradual typing, and software contracts.

faculty.name: Javed Aslam
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Javed Aslam is a professor at Khoury College. His research emphasizes machine learning and information retrieval, with forays into human computation, transportation, computer security, wireless networking, and medical informatics.

faculty.name: David Bau
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: David Bau is an assistant professor at Khoury College and the lead principal investigator of the National Deep Inference Fabric project. His research centers on human–computer interaction and machine learning, including the gap between the efficacy of AI and scientists’ ability to explain it.

faculty.name: Soheil Behnezhad
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Soheil Behnezhad is an associate professor at Khoury College. He is broadly interested in theoretical computer science, with much of his work focused on graph algorithms and the theoretical foundations of big data algorithms.

faculty.name: Jonathan Bell
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Jonathan Bell is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His research focuses on automated approaches to help developers write better software, particularly in Java and JavaScript.

faculty.name: Enrico Bertini
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Enrico Bertini is an associate professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. Through his research and his podcast Data Stories, he works to make data visualization more understandable and useful for broad audiences.

faculty.name: Carla Brodley
faculty.title: Professor, Former Dean
faculty.summary: Professor and former Dean (2014-2021) who hired 46 tenure/tenure-track faculty. Research in computing education, broadening participation in CS, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Seth Cooper
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Seth Cooper is an associate professor at Khoury College. By combining game design with crowdsourcing and automated tools, he has become a pioneer of scientific discovery games.

faculty.name: Gene Cooperman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Gene Cooperman is a professor at Khoury College and leader of the High Performance Computing Laboratory. He researches a slew of interdisciplinary computing, math, and physics topics, and contributed to “GEANT4 – A Simulation Toolkit” — the most widely cited paper in high-energy physics.

faculty.name: Peter Desnoyers
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Peter Desnoyers is an associate professor at Khoury College and a co-founder of the Massachusetts Open Cloud, a multi-institutional collaboration that develops new cloud computing models. His research examines storage issues in operating systems, namely the integration of emerging storage tech into existing software infrastructures.

faculty.name: Jennifer Dy
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Jennifer Dy is a professor in the College of Engineering and Khoury College of Computer Sciences. Her research explores machine learning, data mining, statistical pattern recognition, and computer vision.

faculty.name: Ehsan Elhamifar
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Ehsan Elhamifar is an associate professor at Khoury College, affiliated with the College of Engineering. The overarching goal of his research is to develop AI that learns from and makes inferences about visual data analogous to humans.

faculty.name: Yun Raymond Fu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Yun Raymond Fu is a University Distinguished Professor jointly appointed between the College of Engineering and Khoury College. He is a widely renowned scholar in AI, machine learning, data mining, and computer vision whose resume includes 500+ scientific publications and 40+ patented inventions.

faculty.name: Wolfgang Gatterbauer
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Wolfgang Gatterbauer is an associate professor at Khoury College. He works on the theory of scalable data management, with the goal of expanding data management systems and enabling them to support novel functionalities.

faculty.name: Fatemeh Ghoreishi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Fatemeh Ghoreishi is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Engineering. Her research examines machine learning and Bayesian statistics for design and decision-making under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Arjun Guha
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Arjun Guha is an associate professor at Khoury College. His programming languages research addresses security and reliability problems in web programming, systems, and robotics.

faculty.name: Huaizu Jiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Huaizu Jiang is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His research interests include computer vision, computational photography, machine learning, AI, and natural language processing.

faculty.name: Karl Lieberherr
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Karl Lieberherr is a professor at Khoury College, having joined in 1985. His research has led to advances in programming technology and algorithms that are widely used for object-oriented technology and SAT solvers.

faculty.name: Stacy Marsella
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in computational modeling of cognition, emotion, and social behavior, with applications in virtual agents and human-AI interaction.

faculty.name: Huy Lê Nguyen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Huy Lê Nguyen is an associate professor at Khoury College. He researches the design and analysis of algorithms, with an emphasis on algorithmic techniques for machine learning and massive data sets.

faculty.name: Jose Perea
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Jose Perea is an associate professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Science. He is passionate about using nonstandard mathematical ideas to solve problems in data science and machine learning.

faculty.name: Mirek Riedewald
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Mirek Riedewald is a professor at Khoury College. His research emphasizes the design of novel, scalable data management and analysis techniques, with applications in ornithology, physics, astronomy, and mechanical and aerospace engineering, among other fields.

faculty.name: Ji-Yong Shin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Ji-Yong Shin is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His research focuses on formal verification methods that can be applied to system designs, with additional interests in distributed systems, cloud storage systems, and operating systems.

faculty.name: Frank Tip
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Frank Tip is a professor at Khoury College and a former researcher with IBM and Samsung. He studies a host of software engineering and program analysis topics, including the use of program analysis in tools that make programmers more productive and software more reliable.

faculty.name: Emanuele Viola
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Emanuele “Manu” Viola is a professor at Khoury College. Viola’s theoretical computer science research has included a wide variety of areas, including pseudo-randomness, cryptography, data structures, communication complexity, and circuit lower bounds. He has made fundamental contributions to the field, including solving long-standing open problems.

faculty.name: Jan Vitek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in programming languages, compilers, type systems, and runtime systems.

faculty.name: Malihe Alikhani
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Malihe Alikhani is an assistant professor at Khoury College. Both enthused and wary of the transformative power of AI, Alikhani teaches courses and conducts research on AI ethics and equitable natural language processing.

faculty.name: Christopher Amato
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Christopher Amato is an associate professor at Khoury College and head of the Lab for Learning and Planning in Robotics. His research lies at the intersection of robotics, AI, and machine learning, including planning and reinforcement learning in partially observable and multi-agent/multi-robot systems.

faculty.name: Silvio Amir
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Silvio Amir is an assistant professor at Khoury College. By applying natural language processing, machine learning, and information retrieval methods to personal and user-generated data, he aims to improve the reliability, interpretability, and fairness of predictive models and analytics.

faculty.name: Albert-László Barabási
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Albert-László Barabási is the Robert Gray Dodge Professor of Network Science and a Distinguished University Professor at Northeastern University, director of the Center for Complex Network Research, and a joint appointee within Khoury College and the College of Science. His award-winning work includes the discovery of scale-free networks and the Barabási-Albert model to explain their prevalence in natural, technological, and social systems.

faculty.name: Timothy W. Bickmore
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Timothy Bickmore is a professor at Khoury College and the director of the Relational Agents Group. He develops and evaluates computer agents that emulate face-to-face interactions between health providers and patients, with human–computer interaction, natural language processing, and animation playing a role.

faculty.name: Elettra Bietti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Elettra Bietti is an assistant professor jointly appointed between Khoury College and the School of Law. She became interested in tech while working as an antitrust and intellectual property litigator representing tech and pharmaceutical clients, and now researches how technology overlps with data law, privacy, and antitrust laws in the digital economy.

faculty.name: Terra Blevins
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Terra Blevins is an assistant professor at Khoury College. She aims to answer fundamental questions about the intersections of language and computation, and to leverage those insights to build better human-language technologies.

faculty.name: Michelle Borkin
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Michelle Borkin is an associate professor at Khoury College. She develops novel visualization techniques and tools to enable new data discoveries in fields ranging from cardiology to astronomy.

faculty.name: Carla E. Brodley
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Carla Brodley is the dean of inclusive computing at Khoury College and the founding executive director of Northeastern’s Center for Inclusive Computing, which aims to remove barriers to participation in the field. She was dean of Khoury College from 2014 to 2021.

faculty.name: David Choffnes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: David Choffnes is a professor at Khoury College and a founding member of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. He works to improve the privacy, security, performance, and reliability of internet systems, and designs new models to measure these systems.

faculty.name: Kathleen (Katie) Creel
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Kathleen Creel is an associate professor with joint appointments in the College of Social Sciences and Humanities and Khoury College. Her research explores the moral, political, and epistemic implications of machine learning as it is used in automated decision-making and in science.

faculty.name: Chris Le Dantec
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Christopher Le Dantec is a professor and the director of digital civics at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. His research applies human-centered computing to the intersection of digital democracy and smart cities, including the development of novel computing interfaces and data-driven capacities that support collective action.

faculty.name: Maitraye Das
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Maitraye Das is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. Her human–computer interaction research blends interviews, field work, and iterative design to bridge equity and accessibility gaps in education, employment, and creative work.

faculty.name: Mahsa Derakhshan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Mahsa Derakhshan is an assistant professor at Khoury College. She is broadly interested in the design and analysis of algorithms, specifically algorithms under uncertainty.

faculty.name: Michael Ann DeVito
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Michael Ann DeVito is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. Her AI and machine learning research aims to address inequalities and unfairness toward marginalized populations through inclusive, equitable design.

faculty.name: Cody Dunne
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Cody Dunne is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His work, which lies at the nexus of information visualization, network science, and human–computer interaction, strives to make data and visualizations easier to analyze and share.

faculty.name: Laura Edelson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Laura Edelson is an assistant professor at Khoury College and former chief technologist for the US Department of Justice Antitrust Division. She studies the spread of harmful content through large online networks with the goal of making social media platforms safer and more beneficial for users.

faculty.name: Upol Ehsan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Upon Ehsan is an assistant professor in the Khoury College of Computer Sciences at Northeastern University, based in Boston. His work pioneered the field of Human-centered Explainable AI (HCXAI) from coining the term to cultivating a vibrant research community.

faculty.name: Tina Eliassi-Rad
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Tina Eliassi-Rad is the inaugural Joseph E. Aoun professor at Khoury College, as well as an external faculty member at the Santa Fe Institute and the Vermont Complex Systems Center. Her research at the intersection of data mining, machine learning, and network science has earned her a place as a core faculty member at both Northeastern’s Network Science Institute, and the Institute for Experiential AI.

faculty.name: Mai ElSherief
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Mai ElSherief is an assistant professor at Khoury College. Her research strives to minimize harm and improve prosocial behavior online by detecting and mitigating biases in natural language processing systems.

faculty.name: Michael Everett
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Michael Everett is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Engineering. His research sits at the nexus of robotics, deep learning, and control theory, with the goal of developing certifiable learning machines in which robots can safely, reliably, and efficiently perform tasks.

faculty.name: Don Fallis
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Don Fallis is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. His research blends epistemology, philosophy of information, and philosophy of mathematics, and focuses primarily on adversarial epistemology — how people learn in a disinformation-filled world.

faculty.name: Sina Fazelpour
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Sina Fazelpour is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. His research draws on tools and techniques from philosophy, cognitive science, agent-based simulation, and machine learning to analyze issues of justice, diversity, and reliability in data-driven and AI technologies.

faculty.name: Kevin Fu
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Kevin Fu is a professor at Khoury College and the College of Engineering, and founder and director of the Archimedes Center for Health Care and Medical Device Cybersecurity. He strives to understand and improve the security of embedded systems and devices, particularly in health care.

faculty.name: Joshua Gancher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Joshua Gancher is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His research into cryptographic software and formal methods seeks to mathematically verify the security of foundational software, and to create tools to do that process at scale.

faculty.name: Matthew Goodwin
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Matthew Goodwin is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. He works with people on the autism spectrum to develop and evaluate behavioral assessment and intervention technologies, enabling caregivers to more capably and compassionately assist their loved ones.

faculty.name: Benjamin Gyori
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Benjamin Gyori is the Sy and Laurie Sternberg interdisciplinary associate professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Engineering. His research combines computational modeling, machine learning, natural language processing, and human–machine interaction to improve our understanding of human biology and facilitate advances in health care.

faculty.name: Paul Hand
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Paul Hand is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Science. He researches theory and algorithms for AI and machine learning in the context of vision and imaging.

faculty.name: Megan Hofmann
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Megan Hofmann is an assistant professor at Khoury College. Her human–computer interaction and personal health informatics research often centers around the development and evaluation of accessible tools, including for people with disabilities.

faculty.name: Steve Holtzen
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Steve Holtzen is an assistant professor at Khoury College, affiliated with the Programming Research Laboratory. His research aims to design fast, accessible, and useful probabilistic modeling systems for everyday reasoning tasks, and he teaches courses on artificial intelligence, programming languages, and machine learning.

faculty.name: Lunjia Hu
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant professor at Khoury College working in theoretical computer science and the foundations of machine learning.

faculty.name: Seth Hutchinson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Seth Hutchinson is a professor at Khoury College. His research into ways to make robots smarter and more capable spans three decades and more than 300 publications.

faculty.name: Stephen Intille
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Stephen Intille is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. Using ideas from ubiquitous computing, user-interface design, pattern recognition, behavioral science, and preventative medicine, he develops technologies that measure and motivate health-related behaviors.

faculty.name: Chenyan Jia
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Chenyan Jia is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed in the College of Arts, Media and Design. She conducts research at the intersection of human–computer interaction and mass communication, examining human-centered AI design, the influence of emerging media technologies on human attitudes, and misinformation.

faculty.name: Hang Jiang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Hang Jiang is an assistant professor in Khoury College and the D’Amore-McKim School of Business. His work takes an interdisciplinary approach to a core question: How can AI systems foster a healthy, connected, pluralistic society?

faculty.name: Zhengzhong Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Zhengzhong Jin is an assistant professor at Khoury College. He is interested in cryptography, teaching courses on the subject, and researching a proof system to delegate heavy computation to an untrusted server while ensuring the computation is correct.

faculty.name: Wengong Jin
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Wengong Jin is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His research aims to use geometric and generative AI models to improve the costly, time-consuming process of drug discovery.

faculty.name: Engin Kirda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Engin Kirda is a professor at Khoury College, co-founder of the multinational Secure Systems Lab, and co-founder of Lastline, Inc., which detects and prevents advanced targeted malware. He has published more than 100 papers on malware analysis and detection, web application security, and social networking security.

faculty.name: Rébecca Kleinberger
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Rébecca Kleinberger is an assistant professor at Khoury College. Her research covers topics including both human- and animal-computer interaction; technological enrichment for zoo animals; voice and music technologies; and assistive technology.

faculty.name: Wallace Lages
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Wallace Lages is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. He blends his multidisciplinary research into augmented and virtual reality with his artistic practice, which has been featured on four continents.

faculty.name: David Lazer
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: David Lazer is a University Distinguished Professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. His research investigates misinformation and political communication — especially on social networks — through the lens of computational social science.

faculty.name: Ada Lerner
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Ada Lerner is an assistant professor at Khoury College. She researches human–computer interaction, security, and privacy.

faculty.name: Tianshi Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Tianshi Li is an assistant professor at Khoury College. She has sought to assist developers — even those who don’t specialize in privacy and security — to build mobile apps with native privacy support; she has also helped companies to comply with privacy, accessibility, and fairness requirements.

faculty.name: David Madigan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: David Madigan is a professor at Khoury College and Special Advisor to the President. He has authored over 200 publications in Bayesian statistics, text mining, Monte Carlo methods, pharmacovigilance, and probabilistic graphical models over the course of his career. He was Northeastern’s provost and senior vice president for academic affairs from 2020-2025.

faculty.name: Panagiotis (Pete) Manolios
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Pete Manolios is a professor at Khoury College and the leader of the computer-aided reasoning group. His research uses formal methods to further the design, analysis, and implementation of aerospace, hardware, and component-based software systems.

faculty.name: Stacy C. Marsella
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Stacy Marsella is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Science. His work, which is grounded in the computational modeling of human cognition, emotion, and social behavior, has applications for human behavior, health interventions, social skills training, and planning operations.

faculty.name: Chris Martens
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Chris Martens is an associate professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. A recipient of the NSF Career Award in 2019, they research the design of elegant computational abstractions for interactive and generative software, especially creative applications such as generative art and games.

faculty.name: Varun Mishra
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Varun Mishra is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. His research leverages ubiquitous technologies like smartphones and wearables to enable mental and behavioral health interventions.

faculty.name: Alan Mislove
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Alan Mislove is a professor and the senior associate dean for academic affairs at Khoury College, and a core faculty member of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. His research deals with distributed systems and networks, with a focus on using social networks to enhance the security, privacy, and efficiency of emerging systems.

faculty.name: Cristina Nita-Rotaru
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Cristina Nita-Rotaru is a professor at Khoury College and the executive director and a founding member of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. In her research, she designs and builds secure, resilient distributed systems and network protocols.

faculty.name: Guevara Noubir
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Guevara Noubir is a professor and the executive director of cybersecurity programs at Khoury College. His research examines theoretical and practical aspects of privacy, security, and robustness in networked systems.

faculty.name: Alina Oprea
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Alina Oprea is the assistant dean for faculty affairs and a professor at Khoury College specializing in cloud security, applied cryptography, and security analytics. Over many years in industry and academia, she has researched and designed machine learning techniques to predict and protect against hacker behavior.

faculty.name: Lace Padilla
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Lace Padilla is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Science. She combines cognitive, neural, and computer sciences to study how people process data visualizations and how those visualizations can communicate more accurately and effectively.

faculty.name: Prashant Pandey
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Prashant Pandey is an assistant professor at Khoury College. He researches scalable data systems with robust theoretical foundations for efficient data management, and tackles every level of that challenge, from the theoretical aspects of data structures to the practical issues of scaling data systems.

faculty.name: Rupal Patel
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rupal Patel is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. By analyzing speech production and motor control, then implementing AI and other computation techniques, she designs and develops assistive communication technologies for neurotypical and impaired speakers alike.

faculty.name: Robert Platt
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Rob Platt is an associate professor at Khoury College, affiliated with the College of Engineering. He researches at the intersection of robotic perception, planning, and control, with the aim of enabling robots to perform manipulation tasks in home, factory, warehouse, military, and health care environments.

faculty.name: Predrag Radivojac
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Predrag Radivojac is the associate dean for research and a professor at Khoury College. His work strives to grasp the molecular basis for higher-level phenotypes and genetic disorders, and to develop algorithms and analysis techniques related to the function of biological macromolecules, mass spectrometry proteomics, genome interpretation, and precision health.

faculty.name: Rajmohan Rajaraman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rajmohan Rajamaran is a professor and the associate dean of faculty affairs at Khoury College. He has made important contributions in the areas of distributed hash tables and fundamental combinatorial optimization problems, with the former being incorporated in peer-to-peer systems.

faculty.name: Aanjhan Ranganathan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Aanjhan Ranganathan is an assistant professor and the director of the BS in Cybersecurity program at Khoury College. His research encompasses physical-layer security of wireless systems, secure localization and proximity verification, trusted computing architectures, and side channels.

faculty.name: Xiang (Jenny) Ren
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Xiang (Jenny) Ren is an assistant professor at Khoury College. Her work focuses on building better performance and reliability into system software.

faculty.name: Christoph Riedl
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Christoph Riedl is an associate professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the D’Amore McKim School of Business. His work focuses on optimal team design and management, the impact of social influence and information diffusion on social and economic networks, and the effect those networks have on human collaboration and decision-making.

faculty.name: William Robertson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: William Robertson is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Engineering. Using techniques such as security by design, program analysis, and anomaly detection, he aims to enhance the security of operating systems, mobile devices, and the web.

faculty.name: Herman Saksono
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Herman Saksono is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. His research on human–computer interaction and digital health equity aims to encourage positive behaviors and catalyze social interactions around health, namely through a trio of health apps he designed and developed.

faculty.name: Aarti Sathyanarayana
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Aarti Sathyanarayana is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. Her research leverages signal processing, machine learning algorithms, digital phenotyping, and biomarker discovery to improve human health and performance.

faculty.name: Saiph Savage
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Saiph Savage is an assistant professor and director of the Civic A.I. Lab at Khoury College. Her research focuses on creating intelligent civic technology to organize collective action for change, which includes battling misinformation and empowering gig and rural workers to access better jobs.

faculty.name: Bob De Schutter
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Bob De Schutter is a professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. His game design work emphasizes the importance of older gamers and lifelong play, a mission reflected in his ownership of award-winning game company Lifelong Games LLC.

faculty.name: Abhi Shelat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Abhi Shelat is a professor at Khoury College specializing in cryptography and applied security. A recipient of awards from the NSF, Microsoft, Amazon, Google, and the ACM, he uses secure computation protocols to enable mutually distrusting parties, each with private inputs, to jointly compute functions while ensuring maximal privacy and correctness.

faculty.name: Weiyan Shi
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Weiyan Shi is an assistant professor in the Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Engineering. She is interested in NLP in the context of social influence dialogue systems such as persuasion, negotiation, and recommendation, as well as privacy-preserving NLP applications.

faculty.name: Olin Shivers
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Olin Shivers is a professor at Khoury College. By focusing on the interaction between systems and higher-order typed programming languages, he can assist programmers in building robust, complex software.

faculty.name: David Smith
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: David Smith is an associate professor at Khoury College. His research spans the fields of natural language processing, computational linguistics, information retrieval, machine learning, digital libraries, digital humanities, and political science.

faculty.name: Ravi Sundaram
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Ravi Sundaram is a professor at Khoury College and the former director of engineering at Akamai Technologies, where he helped build the world’s leading content delivery network. In his network and algorithms research, he devises efficient schemes to improve the performance of network-based applications.

faculty.name: Cheng Tan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Cheng Tan is an assistant professor at Khoury College. His systems and security research focuses on building verifiable outsourced services and certified neural networks.

faculty.name: Zhi Tan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Zhi Tan is an assistant professor at Khoury College. He studies how robots interact with the world and how they can be integrated with human users, each other, and intelligent systems.

faculty.name: Alexandra To
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Alexandra To is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Arts, Media and Design. Her human–computer interaction research aims to uplift marginalized people, allowing them to access joy, play, and justice through tech.

faculty.name: Lorenzo Torresani
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Lorenzo Torresani is a professor and the President Joseph E. Aoun Chair at Khoury College. He is passionate about creating perceptual AI agents that go beyond recognizing user actions to understand how activities are performed and assist humans in daily tasks.

faculty.name: Stavros Tripakis
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Stavros Tripakis is an associate professor at Khoury College. His research centers on formal methods, computer-aided verification and synthesis, security, and safety-critical, embedded, and cyber-physical systems.

faculty.name: Rebekah Tromble
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Rebekah Tromble is a professor at Khoury College and the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. She is a widely recognized expert in the responsible use of sociotechnical tools to connect public servants, researchers, and the wider community, and is particularly interested in extremist activity online.

faculty.name: Jonathan Ullman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Jonathan Ullman is an associate professor at Khoury College whose research centers on the foundations of privacy for machine learning and statistics. Ullman has been recognized with an NSF CAREER award and the Ruth and Joel Spira Outstanding Teacher Award.

faculty.name: Alessandro Vespignani
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Alessandro Vespignani is the Sternberg Distinguished University Professor, and an interdisciplinary appointee between Khoury College and the Bouvé College of Health Sciences. He uses statistical and numerical simulation methods to study the behavior of complex biological, social, and technological networks.

faculty.name: Olga Vitek
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Olga Vitek is the Raymond Bradford Bradstreet Professor at Khoury College, and the director of the Barnett Institute for Chemical and Biological Analysis. Her lab, which has been recognized with multiple major awards, uses statistical science, machine learning, and large-scale mass spectrometry to understand the functioning of living organisms.

faculty.name: Byron Wallace
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Byron Wallace is the director for the undergraduate artificial intelligence program and the Sy and Laurie Sternberg Interdisciplinary Associate Professor at Khoury College. He applies machine learning and natural language processing methods in the health informatics space, with the goal of developing hybrid human–AI systems and streamlining the synthesis of biomedical information.

faculty.name: Robin Walters
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Robin Walters is an assistant professor at Khoury College. He leads the Geometric Learning Lab, where his research explores the role symmetry can play in developing data-efficient, trustworthy deep learning models.

faculty.name: Dakuo Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Dakuo Wang is an associate professor at Khoury College. He is also an ACM Distinguished Speaker and gives talks around the world on his research into human-centered AI (HCAI) systems.

faculty.name: Daniel Wichs
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Daniel Wichs is a professor at Khoury College. An expert in modern cryptography, Wichs researches all aspects of the field, including its theoretical foundations and its applications to information security. Wichs’ work was recognized in 2018 with the prestigious Sloan Research Fellowship, which honors early-career scholars whose achievements mark them among the top scientific minds.

faculty.name: Christo Wilson
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Christo Wilson is an associate professor and associate dean of undergraduate programs at Khoury College. His research, which draws on computational, political, and economic methods, delves into the data, security, and privacy issues at the heart of our internet use.

faculty.name: Lawson Wong
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Lawson Wong is an associate professor at Khoury College. His research focuses on learning, representing, and estimating knowledge about environments and the world in ways that autonomous robots can use.

faculty.name: Xiao Yang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Tenure-track assistant professor at Khoury College; a detailed public research profile is not yet available.

faculty.name: Shuo Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Shuo Zhang is an assistant professor at Khoury College, jointly appointed with the College of Social Sciences and Humanities. Her research examines how labor economics, platform design, algorithmic fairness, and human behavior influence online job markets.

faculty.name: Hongyang Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Hongyang Zhang is an assistant professor at Khoury College. He researches at the nexus of machine learning, algorithms, and statistics, and has helped to develop techniques for neural networks, data augmentation, and transfer learning.

faculty.name: Ziming Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Ziming Zhao is an associate professor at Khoury College. His passion for hacking informs his research into systems and software security, network security, and web security, as well as his use of capture the flag (CTF) cybersecurity competitions as a teaching tool.

faculty.name: Abhi Shelat
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in cryptography. Ph.D. from MIT. Previously tenured at University of Virginia. NSF CAREER award, Microsoft Faculty Fellowship. Research in applied cryptography and secure computation.

faculty.name: Daniel Wichs
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor in cryptography. Research on computing on encrypted data, program obfuscation, lattice-based cryptography, and information-theoretic cryptography. Senior Scientist at NTT Research.

faculty.name: Alina Oprea
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, including adversarial ML, trustworthy AI, privacy in ML, security for LLM agents, and cloud security. Google Security and Privacy Award (2019).

faculty.name: Cristina Nita-Rotaru
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and founding member of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in security of network protocols and distributed systems, adversarial testing, formal verification, and blockchains. NSF CAREER (2006).

faculty.name: Engin Kirda
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in systems, software, and network security, malware detection and analysis, and web application security.

faculty.name: David Choffnes
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research to improve the privacy, security, performance, and reliability of internet systems. Former Executive Director of the Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Alan Mislove
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in network measurement and privacy/security in online social networks.

faculty.name: Christo Wilson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in algorithm auditing, online tracking, privacy, and platform accountability.

faculty.name: Guevara Noubir
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in privacy and security, robustness of networked systems, and wireless security.

faculty.name: Jonathan Ullman
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor researching foundations of privacy for ML and statistics, differential privacy, and cryptography. Member of Theory Group and Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Long Lu
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor and co-director of SecLab. Research in low-level software security, embedded and IoT/CPS systems security, program analysis, and trusted computing. NSF CAREER awardee.

faculty.name: Ziming Zhao
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in systems and software security, network and web security, and human-centric security. NSF CAREER Award, ACM SACMAT Test-of-Time Award 2024.

faculty.name: Tianshi Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Directs the PEACH (Privacy-Enabling AI and Computer-Human interaction) Lab. Core faculty at Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in HCI, privacy, AI, and LLM privacy issues.

faculty.name: Jayshree Sarathy
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Member of Cybersecurity and Privacy Institute. Research in privacy, data science, and sociotechnical dynamics of responsible data science.

faculty.name: Laura Edelson
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor in cybersecurity and privacy and human-centered computing. Studies the spread of harmful content through large online networks.

faculty.name: Kevin Fu
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with College of Engineering
faculty.summary: Professor jointly appointed with Engineering. Research seeks to improve the security of embedded systems and devices, particularly in healthcare. Pioneer in medical device cybersecurity.

faculty.name: Joshua Gancher
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Aims to mathematically verify the security of foundational software and create tools to do that process at scale.

faculty.name: Elettra Bietti
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Jointly with School of Law
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor jointly appointed with Law. Researches how technology overlaps with data law, privacy, and antitrust laws in the digital economy.

faculty.name: Don Fallis
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with CSSH
faculty.summary: Professor jointly appointed with CSSH. Studies adversarial epistemology -- how people learn in a disinformation-filled world.

faculty.name: Timothy W. Bickmore
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor and Director of the Relational Agents Group. Develops computer agents for health provider-patient interactions. Research in HCI, natural language processing, and animation for health counseling. Ph.D. from MIT Media Lab (2003). NSF CAREER awardee. Top 2% most-cited scientists worldwide.

faculty.name: Elizabeth Mynatt
faculty.title: Dean and Professor
faculty.summary: Dean of Khoury College. Research in human-computer interaction, health informatics, and ubiquitous computing with focus on aging-in-place and chronic disease self-management.

faculty.name: Stephen Intille
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.sub_field: Personal Health Technologies and Wearable Sensing
faculty.summary: Professor. Research on personal health technologies, wearable sensing, behavioral informatics, and mobile health interventions. Develops sensor-based systems for continuous health monitoring.

faculty.name: Aarti Sathyanarayana
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in digital health, wearable devices, digital biomarkers, and machine learning for health monitoring.

faculty.name: Herman Saksono
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in human-centered computing and health informatics, with focus on health equity and community-based health interventions.

faculty.name: Dakuo Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in HCI, human-AI collaboration, NLP, and health informatics. Focus on designing AI systems that support healthcare work.

faculty.name: Predrag Radivojac
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in bioinformatics, machine learning, and proteomics applied to health informatics and precision medicine. Develops computational methods for biological and clinical data.

faculty.name: Maitraye Das
faculty.title: Assistant Professor, Jointly with CAMD
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor jointly with CAMD. HCI research on equity and accessibility gaps in education, employment, and creative work, including health-related contexts.

faculty.name: Armando Cortez
faculty.title: Part-Time Lecturer, Health Informatics
faculty.summary: Part-Time Lecturer and CIO at Harbor Health Services. Focus on co-evolution of healthcare and technology. Brings industry perspective on health informatics.

faculty.name: Byron Wallace
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in data mining, machine learning, and NLP with applications to health informatics and evidence-based medicine. Focus on automating systematic reviews and clinical text mining.

faculty.name: Albert-László Barabási
faculty.title: Robert Gray Dodge Professor of Network Science, University Distinguished Professor
faculty.summary: Distinguished Professor who discovered scale-free networks and developed the Barabási-Albert preferential attachment model. Director of Center for Complex Network Research. Elected to National Academy of Sciences (2024). Appointments at Harvard Medical School and Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. Author of 'Linked' and 'The Formula'.

faculty.name: Tina Eliassi-Rad
faculty.title: Inaugural Joseph E. Aoun Professor
faculty.summary: Inaugural Joseph E. Aoun Professor. Core faculty of Network Science Institute and Institute for Experiential AI. Research at intersection of data mining, machine learning, and network science.

faculty.name: David Lazer
faculty.title: University Distinguished Professor, Jointly with CSSH
faculty.sub_field: Computational Social Science and Network Science
faculty.summary: University Distinguished Professor and core faculty of the Network Science Institute. Research in computational social science and network science, studying social and political networks at scale.

faculty.name: Cody Dunne
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Associate Professor. Research in information visualization, network science, and human-computer interaction. Strives to make network data and visualizations easier to analyze and share.

faculty.name: Alan Mislove
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in network measurement and online social networks, studying the structure and dynamics of large-scale online platforms.

faculty.name: Christo Wilson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in algorithm auditing, online platform dynamics, and how algorithmic systems shape networked information flows.

faculty.name: Christoph Riedl
faculty.title: Professor, Jointly with D'Amore-McKim School of Business
faculty.summary: Professor. Research in collective intelligence, human-AI teaming, and crowdsourcing, studying how networked collaboration shapes outcomes.

faculty.name: Alessandro Vespignani
faculty.title: Sternberg Family Distinguished University Professor, Jointly with COS
faculty.summary: Sternberg Family Distinguished University Professor. Research in complex networks, computational epidemiology, and epidemic modeling. Core faculty of the Network Science Institute.

faculty.name: Ricardo Baeza-Yates
faculty.title: Professor of the Practice
faculty.summary: Professor of the Practice. Research in information retrieval, web search, AI, and web science with applications to network analysis. Former VP of Research at Yahoo.

faculty.name: Mahsa Derakhshan
faculty.title: Assistant Professor
faculty.summary: Assistant Professor. Research in design and analysis of algorithms, specifically algorithms under uncertainty with applications to networked systems.

faculty.name: Samir Khuller
faculty.title: Peter and Adrienne Barris Chair of Computer Science, Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Khuller leads the Computer Science department and works on the design and analysis of combinatorial and approximation algorithms, including graph algorithms, scheduling, and network design.

faculty.name: Kristian Hammond
faculty.title: Bill and Cathy Osborn Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Hammond works on artificial intelligence and natural language generation, building systems that reason over data and communicate results in natural language.

faculty.name: Ken Forbus
faculty.title: Walter P. Murphy Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Forbus works on qualitative reasoning, analogical reasoning, and cognitive systems, building AI systems that model human commonsense and conceptual reasoning.

faculty.name: Han Liu
faculty.title: Orrington Lunt Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Industrial Engineering & Management Sciences
faculty.summary: Liu works on statistical machine learning, including high-dimensional and nonparametric inference, optimization, and the theoretical foundations of deep learning.

faculty.name: Jason Hartline
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Hartline works on economics and computation, including auction and mechanism design, approximation in economic settings, and the theory of markets.

faculty.name: Jessica Hullman
faculty.title: Ginni Rometty Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Hullman researches data visualization and human-computer interaction, focusing on how people reason with data and uncertainty and how to design visual and statistical communication.

faculty.name: Peter Dinda
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Dinda's group works on experimental computer systems, including operating systems, parallel and high-performance computing, and virtualization.

faculty.name: Fabian Bustamante
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Bustamante's group studies large-scale distributed and networked systems, with an emphasis on internet and mobile network measurement and performance.

faculty.name: Aleksandar Kuzmanovic
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Kuzmanovic researches computer networking and networked systems, including internet architecture, measurement, and the interaction of networks with applications.

faculty.name: Nikos Hardavellas
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science, Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Hardavellas researches computer architecture and systems, focusing on memory systems and energy-efficient parallel architectures for data-intensive workloads.

faculty.name: Edith Elkind
faculty.title: Ginni Rometty Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Elkind studies algorithmic game theory and computational social choice, including voting, coalition formation, and fair division in multiagent systems.

faculty.name: Konstantin Makarychev
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Makarychev works on theoretical computer science, particularly approximation algorithms, clustering, and combinatorial optimization.

faculty.name: Aravindan Vijayaraghavan
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Industrial Engineering & Management Sciences
faculty.summary: Vijayaraghavan works on theoretical computer science and the foundations of machine learning, including approximation algorithms, clustering, and provable methods for learning.

faculty.name: V.S. Subrahmanian
faculty.title: Walter P. Murphy Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Subrahmanian works on artificial intelligence and security, including machine learning and data analytics for cybersecurity, counterterrorism, and computational policy.

faculty.name: Xinyu Xing
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Xing researches systems and software security, including automated vulnerability discovery and analysis and the security of machine-learning systems.

faculty.name: Xiao Wang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Wang researches cryptography and security, specializing in efficient secure multiparty computation, zero-knowledge proofs, and their practical implementations.

faculty.name: Robby Findler
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Findler is a core developer of the Racket programming language and works on programming languages, software contracts, and language-based development tools.

faculty.name: Christos Dimoulas
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Dimoulas studies programming languages, including type systems, software contracts, and the design of languages that enforce correctness and security properties.

faculty.name: Matthew Kay
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Associate Professor of Communication Studies
faculty.summary: Kay works on human-computer interaction and data visualization, including uncertainty visualization and tools that help people interpret data and statistical information.

faculty.name: Brenna Argall
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science, Professor of Mechanical Engineering, Professor of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation
faculty.summary: Argall's group develops assistive and rehabilitation robotics, including shared-control and machine-learning methods that adapt robotic devices to users with motor impairments.

faculty.name: Emma Alexander
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Alexander works at the intersection of computer vision and optics, designing computational imaging systems and studying depth and light-based sensing inspired by biological vision.

faculty.name: Manling Li
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Li works on natural language processing and multimodal AI, including information and knowledge extraction and language-model agents that reason across text, vision, and structured knowledge.

faculty.name: Miklos Racz
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Associate Professor of Statistics
faculty.summary: Racz works on probability, statistics, and network science, including inference on random graphs and the mathematics of large networked systems.

faculty.name: Simone Campanoni
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Campanoni works on compilers and computer architecture, developing program-analysis and code-optimization techniques that automatically extract parallelism and performance.

faculty.name: Bryan Pardo
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Pardo leads work on machine listening and audio, developing machine-learning and interactive tools for music and sound analysis, separation, and production.

faculty.name: Karan Ahuja
faculty.title: Lisa Wissner-Slivka and Benjamin Slivka Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Ahuja studies human-computer interaction and ubiquitous computing, developing sensing systems and machine learning methods that let devices perceive human activity and context.

faculty.name: Nivedita Arora
faculty.title: Allen K. and Johnnie Cordell Breed Junior Professor of Design, Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Assistant Professor of Computer Scien
faculty.summary: Arora designs sustainable, self-powered interactive computing devices and materials, spanning human-computer interaction, embedded sensing, and low-power hardware.

faculty.name: Larry Birnbaum
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Birnbaum works on artificial intelligence, natural language processing, and intelligent information systems, including natural language generation and machine-assisted content.

faculty.name: Yan Chen
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Chen's research spans computer networking and security, including network measurement, intrusion and malware detection, and security of mobile and IoT systems.

faculty.name: Alok Choudhary
faculty.title: Harold Washington Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Computer Science
faculty.summary: Choudhary works on high-performance and parallel computing, large-scale data mining, and data-intensive scientific computing.

faculty.name: Andrew Crotty
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Crotty builds database and data-management systems, focusing on high-performance query processing and analytics engines.

faculty.name: Douglas Downey
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Downey works on natural language processing and machine learning, including information extraction, language models, and AI methods applied to scientific text.

faculty.name: Michael Horn
faculty.title: Charles Deering McCormick Professor of Teaching Excellence, Professor of Computer Science, Professor of Education and Social Policy
faculty.summary: Horn designs interactive and tangible computing technologies for learning, spanning human-computer interaction, educational technology, and the learning sciences.

faculty.name: Ian Horswill
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Horswill works on artificial intelligence for interactive systems and games, including behavior-based and cognitively inspired AI for characters and agents.

faculty.name: Maia Jacobs
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science, Assistant Professor of Preventive Medicine
faculty.summary: Jacobs designs human-centered computing and AI tools for health, studying how patients and clinicians interact with health technologies and decision-support systems.

faculty.name: Russell Joseph
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Joseph researches computer architecture, with an emphasis on power, thermal, and reliability management in processors and hardware systems.

faculty.name: Sam Kriegman
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science, Assistant Professor of Mechanical Engineering, Assistant Professor of Chemical and Biological Engineering
faculty.summary: Kriegman studies embodied AI and robotics, using evolutionary and machine-learning methods to design bodies and behaviors of soft, biohybrid, and reconfigurable robots.

faculty.name: Sidhanth Mohanty
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Mohanty works in theoretical computer science on spectral and average-case algorithms, random constraint satisfaction, and the sum-of-squares hierarchy.

faculty.name: Seda Ogrenci
faculty.title: Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Ogrenci researches reconfigurable and embedded computing, including FPGA-based systems and hardware acceleration for machine learning at the edge.

faculty.name: Eleanor O'Rourke
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Associate Professor of Education and Social Policy
faculty.summary: O'Rourke designs learning technologies and studies human-computer interaction in education, including tools that support student motivation and growth mindset.

faculty.name: Chris Riesbeck
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Riesbeck works on artificial intelligence and cognitive science, including case-based reasoning and learning-by-doing approaches to education.

faculty.name: Jennie Rogers
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Rogers builds database and data-management systems, with work on federated databases and privacy-preserving query processing across multiple data owners.

faculty.name: Michael Rubenstein
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Associate Professor of Mechanical Engineering
faculty.summary: Rubenstein researches swarm and multi-robot systems, developing large collectives of simple robots that coordinate to achieve self-assembly and collective behavior.

faculty.name: Kate Smith
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science and (by courtesy) Electrical and Computer Engineering
faculty.summary: Smith works on quantum computing systems, including architectures, compilation, and system-software techniques for near-term quantum hardware.

faculty.name: Uri Wilensky
faculty.title: Professor of Computer Science, Professor of Education and Social Policy, Lorraine Morton Professor
faculty.summary: Wilensky created the NetLogo modeling environment and studies agent-based modeling of complex systems and computationally rich approaches to STEM education.

faculty.name: Marcelo Worsley
faculty.title: Karr Family Associate Professor of Computer Science, Associate Professor of Learning Sciences
faculty.summary: Worsley works on multimodal learning analytics and human-computer interaction, using sensing and machine learning to understand and support hands-on and inclusive learning.

faculty.name: Haoqi Zhang
faculty.title: Associate Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Zhang works on human-computer interaction and social computing, designing crowdsourcing and community systems that coordinate people to accomplish complex goals.

faculty.name: Ruohan Zhang
faculty.title: Assistant Professor of Computer Science (beginning September 1, 2026)
faculty.summary: Zhang, an incoming assistant professor, works on reinforcement and imitation learning for robotics and embodied AI, including learning from human attention and demonstration.

funding.note: Fully funded: full tuition, health plan, and base stipend for all Ph.D. students throughout regular enrollment

faculty.name: Sanjeev Arora
faculty.title: Charles C. Fitzmorris Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theoretical computer science and the theoretical foundations of machine learning and deep learning.

faculty.name: Jennifer Rexford
faculty.title: Gordon Y.S. Wu Professor of Engineering
faculty.summary: Researches computer networking, including software-defined networking, routing, and network programmability.

faculty.name: Robert Tarjan
faculty.title: James S. McDonnell Distinguished University Professor
faculty.summary: Turing Award winner (1986). Inventor of foundational graph algorithms (Tarjan's SCC, union-find, Fibonacci heaps) and seminal contributions to algorithm design and data structures. NAE member.

faculty.name: Margaret Martonosi
faculty.title: William M. Addy '82 University Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer architecture and systems, including quantum computing systems and energy-efficient/mobile computing. Serves as Associate Chair.

faculty.name: Arvind Narayanan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on information privacy, security, and the societal implications of AI and algorithms.

faculty.name: Olga Troyanskaya
faculty.title: Maduraperuma/Khot Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Researches computational biology and genomics, using machine learning to model gene networks and disease.

faculty.name: Michael Freedman
faculty.title: Robert E. Kahn Professor of Computer Science
faculty.summary: Researches distributed systems and networking, including scalable cloud and storage systems.

faculty.name: Elad Hazan
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on machine learning and mathematical optimization, including online learning, regret minimization, and control.

faculty.name: Ryan Adams
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches machine learning, including probabilistic modeling, Bayesian inference, and ML for scientific and engineering applications.

faculty.name: Jia Deng
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches computer vision and machine learning; co-creator of the ImageNet dataset and work on visual recognition.

faculty.name: Karthik Narasimhan
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches natural language processing and reinforcement learning, including language understanding and language-guided agents.

faculty.name: Danqi Chen
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Researches natural language processing and machine learning, including representation learning, question answering, and language models.

faculty.name: Kyle Jamieson
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on wireless and mobile networking, indoor localization, and emerging quantum networking. Serves as Director of Graduate Studies.

faculty.name: Wyatt Lloyd
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches distributed systems, including consistency, caching, and storage for large-scale services. Serves as Associate Chair.

faculty.name: Mark Braverman
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Researches theoretical computer science, including information and communication complexity and algorithmic game theory.

faculty.name: Ran Raz
faculty.title: Professor
faculty.summary: Works on computational complexity theory, including circuit and communication complexity and quantum computation.

faculty.name: Gillat Kol
faculty.title: Associate Professor
faculty.summary: Works on theoretical computer science, including communication complexity, interactive coding, and distributed algorithms.

faculty.name: Olga Russakovsky
faculty.title: Associate Professor

faculty.name: Ashok Veeraraghavan
